name: ConsumerConfiguration
documentation:
Configuration class that defines how a message consumer behaves during message tracking and handler invocation.

{@code ConsumerConfiguration} is used to fine-tune the behavior of message consumers beyond what is possible with the {@link Consumer} annotation. It supports handler filtering, tracking concurrency, custom interceptors, and more.

Usage: Consumers can be declared programmatically using this configuration object, generated automatically from {@code @Consumer} annotations on handler classes or packages, or derived from the default consumer template for handlers without an explicit consumer. @see Consumer @see io.fluxzero.sdk.configuration.FluxzeroBuilder#addConsumerConfiguration

fields.name: DEFAULT_APP_CONSUMER_MODE
fields.documentation: Assigns unconfigured handlers to the shared default application consumer for their message type.

fields.name: DEFAULT_HANDLING_MODE_BY_MESSAGE_TYPE_PROPERTY_PREFIX
fields.documentation:
Prefix for message-type specific default handler execution mode properties.

For example, {@code fluxzero.tracking.defaultHandlingMode.webrequest = async} changes only web request default consumers while {@link #DEFAULT_HANDLING_MODE_PROPERTY} still applies app-wide.

fields.name: DEFAULT_HANDLING_MODE_PROPERTY
fields.documentation:
App-wide default handler execution mode used when a consumer and its message-type default consumer both use
 {@link ConsumerHandlingMode#DEFAULT}.

fields.name: DEFAULT_MAX_FETCH_BYTES
fields.documentation: Default serialized payload byte limit per tracking fetch.

fields.name: MAX_FETCH_BYTES_PROPERTY
fields.documentation:
Configures the default serialized payload byte limit per tracking fetch. Consumers can override this default
 with {@link Builder#maxFetchBytes(long)} or {@link Consumer#maxFetchBytes()}.

fields.name: PER_HANDLER_CONSUMER_MODE
fields.documentation: Creates an isolated default consumer per unconfigured handler class.

fields.name: UNCONFIGURED_HANDLER_CONSUMER_MODE_PROPERTY
fields.documentation:
Chooses how unconfigured tracking handlers are assigned to default consumers.

Supported values are {@link #PER_HANDLER_CONSUMER_MODE} and {@link #DEFAULT_APP_CONSUMER_MODE}. When absent, Fluxzero derives the default from {@link ApplicationProperties#DEFAULTS_VERSION_PROPERTY}.

fields.name: USE_DEFAULT_MAX_FETCH_BYTES
fields.documentation:
Sentinel value for consumers that inherit the configured default max fetch byte limit. This value is resolved
 before read requests are sent.

fields.name: awaitAsyncResults
fields.documentation:
If true, asynchronous handler results are awaited before the consumer completes the current batch.

When false, asynchronous results are still published when they complete, but the consumer can store its position and fetch the next batch without waiting for those futures. This is the historical default and is useful for high-throughput request handlers where each request has its own response correlation and batch-level ordering is not needed.

fields.name: awaitSendAndForgetFutures
fields.documentation:
If true, futures returned by fire-and-forget dispatches started during this consumer's batch processing are awaited before the consumer stores its position.

This lets handlers use {@code sendAndForget(..., Guarantee.STORED)} without explicitly joining the returned future just to ensure the dispatch has reached its guarantee before the tracker commits progress. Disable this for consumers that intentionally let fire-and-forget dispatches complete independently from batch commits. Failures while awaiting are handled by this consumer's error handler like other batch processing failures.

fields.name: batchInterceptors
fields.documentation:
Interceptors that are invoked before and after a batch of messages is processed.

These interceptors are ordered within the consumer using {@link io.fluxzero.sdk.common.Order @Order}. When a tracker is started with a {@link io.fluxzero.sdk.Fluxzero} instance, the ordered consumer interceptors wrap the globally configured batch interceptors for that message type.

fields.name: clientControlledIndex
fields.documentation:
If true, the consumer's position in the log will be controlled by the client (not automatically). Allows handling
 messages manually or out of order.

fields.name: dispatchInterceptors
fields.documentation:
Dispatch interceptors that become active while this consumer is processing a batch.

These interceptors are ordered within the consumer using {@link io.fluxzero.sdk.common.Order @Order} and are applied through {@link io.fluxzero.sdk.publishing.AdhocDispatchInterceptor}. They therefore affect dispatches performed by this consumer without changing the global dispatch chain. If ad hoc dispatch interceptors are disabled globally, these consumer-scoped dispatch interceptors are inactive.

fields.name: effectiveMaxFetchBytes
fields.documentation: Effective byte limit used for read requests.

fields.name: errorHandler
fields.documentation:
Defines how errors during handler invocation are handled. Defaults to logging errors and continuing with other
 messages.

fields.name: exclusive
fields.documentation:
If true (default), ensures this consumer is exclusive — no other consumers with the same name will run
 concurrently.

fields.name: exclusiveAfterMaxIndex
fields.documentation:
Whether this consumer remains exclusive for shared handlers from {@link #getMaxIndexExclusive()} onward.

Intended primarily for consumer merge or handover scenarios. Set this to {@code false} on the retiring consumer when another consumer should take over once {@code maxIndexExclusive} has been reached.

fields.name: exclusiveBeforeMinIndex
fields.documentation:
Whether this consumer remains exclusive for shared handlers before {@link #getMinIndex()}.

Intended primarily for consumer split scenarios. Set this to {@code false} on the new consumer when it should only become exclusively responsible from {@code minIndex} onward, while another consumer continues to handle older messages.

fields.name: filterMessageTarget
fields.documentation:
If true, only messages that target the current application will be processed. This allows isolating handlers to
 specific applications.

fields.name: flowRegulator
fields.documentation:
Regulates the flow of messages to the consumer to prevent overload. Defaults to {@link NoOpFlowRegulator}, which
 performs no throttling.

fields.name: handlerFilter
fields.documentation:
Optional predicate that determines whether a given handler is included in this consumer.

This is especially useful when combining multiple handler classes or packages and you want to restrict consumption to a subset.

fields.name: handlerInterceptors
fields.documentation:
Interceptors that wrap handler execution for additional behavior (e.g. logging, metrics, authorization).

These interceptors are ordered within the consumer using {@link io.fluxzero.sdk.common.Order @Order}. Global handler interceptors configured via {@code FluxzeroBuilder} keep their existing position in the chain; consumer interceptors are only sorted relative to other interceptors in the same consumer configuration.

fields.name: handlingMode
fields.documentation:
Controls whether handlers assigned to this consumer execute in the tracker thread or on a worker thread.

{@link ConsumerHandlingMode#DEFAULT} inherits the message-type default consumer's resolved mode. If that is also default, Fluxzero falls back to {@link #defaultHandlingModeProperty(MessageType)}, {@link #DEFAULT_HANDLING_MODE_PROPERTY}, and versioned SDK defaults.

fields.name: ignoreSegment
fields.documentation:
If true, the consumer will ignore the segment assigned to it and process all messages regardless of partitioning.
 Useful for global handlers such as notifications or singletons.

fields.name: maxFetchBytes
fields.documentation:
Maximum serialized payload bytes to fetch per poll from the message log.

The default {@link #USE_DEFAULT_MAX_FETCH_BYTES} inherits {@link #MAX_FETCH_BYTES_PROPERTY} when configured, otherwise {@link #DEFAULT_MAX_FETCH_BYTES}. A value of {@code 0} disables this limit. If a single message is larger than this limit, it may still be returned alone so tracking can make progress.

fields.name: maxFetchSize
fields.documentation: Maximum number of messages to fetch per poll from the message log.

fields.name: maxIndexExclusive
fields.documentation: Optional exclusive upper limit for message index (messages with this index or higher will be skipped).

fields.name: maxWaitDuration
fields.documentation:
Maximum wait time for polling new messages. Use {@link Duration#ZERO} to return immediately when no messages or
 segment claim are available.

fields.name: minIndex
fields.documentation: Optional minimum index to start processing messages from.

fields.name: name
fields.documentation: Unique name for the consumer. Used for tracking and identifying its state.

fields.name: namespace
fields.documentation:
Specifies the namespace under which the consumer tracks messages.

If set to {@code null}, the default namespace of the client application is used. @see Client#forNamespace(String)

fields.name: onMissingProtectedData
fields.documentation:
Default response when a handler in this consumer receives a message whose protected data is no longer available.
 Individual handler annotations may override this setting.

fields.name: passive
fields.documentation:
If true, the consumer will passively listen to messages without marking them as consumed. Useful for debug,
 logging, or metrics purposes.

fields.name: purgeDelay
fields.documentation: Optional delay after which previously consumed segments should be purged from memory.

fields.name: singleTracker
fields.documentation: If true, forces a single tracker to be created for this consumer regardless of segment count.

fields.name: storePositionManually
fields.documentation:
Whether this consumer is taking manual control over storing its position in the log.

When {@code true}, the consumer is responsible for explicitly storing its position after processing one or more message batches. This allows for greater control — for example, when handling long-running workflows that span multiple batches, or when committing position should be deferred until post-processing is complete.

When {@code false} (the default), the position is automatically updated after each message batch is processed, ensuring progress is recorded and avoiding reprocessing on restart.

Note: Even with manual position tracking enabled, the consumer will continue to receive "new" messages as long as the tracking process remains active. However, its persisted position will not be updated unless explicitly stored.

fields.name: threads
fields.documentation: Number of concurrent threads to process messages.

fields.name: trackerIdFactory
fields.documentation:
Factory for generating a tracker ID for this consumer. The default implementation appends a random UUID to the
 client ID.

fields.name: typeFilter
fields.documentation:
Optional class name pattern to filter message types. When provided, only messages whose payload matches the type
 name pattern will be processed.

methods.name: configurations
methods.documentation:
Returns a stream of {@code ConsumerConfiguration}s by inspecting the given handler classes and their packages.
 Includes both class-level and package-level {@code @Consumer} annotations.

methods.name: defaultHandlingModeProperty
ordered
methods.documentation: Returns a copy with consumer-specific interceptors ordered by {@link io.fluxzero.sdk.common.Order}.
